instruction:
Given that the side length of square $ABDC$ is $a$, try to find a point $E$ such that after removing the isosceles triangle $AEB$ from the square, point $E$ becomes the centroid of the remaining area . So where is the position of point E ?

Options:
A. $$
\frac{a} {2}, 0. 8 3 4 a
$$
B. $$
\frac{a} {7}, 0. 6 4 1 a
$$
C. $$
\frac{a} {2}, 0. 6 9 2 a
$$
D. $$
\frac{a} {3}, 0. 5 7 8 a
$$
E. $$
\frac{a} {6}, 0. 7 3 4 a
$$
F. $$
\frac{a} {4}, 0. 7 6 2 a
$$
G. $$
\frac{a} {2}, 0. 6 3 4 a
$$
H. $$
\frac{a} {5}, 0. 6 3 4 a
$$
I. $$
\frac{a} {2}, 0. 6 5 8 a
$$
J. $$
\frac{a} {2}, 0. 5 9 6 a
$$
